_README: Gate-evaluation dataset. 4 real non-AMINA crypto/fintech firms. For each: the bank's ONBOARDING-baseline beliefs (what was true ~2-3y ago) + a frozen set of REAL public events (gathered 2026-06, with source URLs) that drift those beliefs. Events are typed the way our connectors actually emit them (mostly `news` with a text summary — the HARD case for a keyword gate). `gold.json` holds Claude's reference labels. NOTHING here calls an API; the harness runs the real `is_candidate` gate (pure rules) against the gold labels.

companies.onboarded_as: US-focused regulated stablecoin issuer (USDC), private company

beliefs.business_model: US-based stablecoin issuer (USDC) and payments infrastructure
beliefs.product_mix: USDC stablecoin issuance and payments APIs; no banking, no lending
beliefs.regulatory_status: US state money-transmitter licences + NYDFS BitLicense; NOT a bank
beliefs.ubo: Founders Jeremy Allaire and Sean Neville; venture investors; privately held
beliefs.source_of_wealth: Venture funding and stablecoin reserve income
beliefs.digital_asset_policy: Issues USDC; reserves held in cash and short-dated US Treasuries
beliefs.pep_status: No PEP among directors or UBOs
beliefs.adverse_media_status: No adverse media, investigation or litigation

events.summary: Circle becomes first global stablecoin issuer to obtain a MiCA-compliant Electronic Money Institution licence in France, expanding into the EU
Circle goes public on the NYSE at $31 per share under ticker CRCL in a $5B IPO
GENIUS Act signed into US law; defines payment stablecoins, mandates 1:1 reserves, monthly attestations and annual audits for large issuers
Circle receives OCC conditional approval to establish First National Digital Currency Bank, N.A., a national trust bank to hold the USDC reserve
Circle now issues both dollar- and euro-denominated stablecoins compliant with the EU MiCA regime, the only major global issuer to do so

companies.onboarded_as: Crypto cross-border payments company (XRP), private, under SEC litigation

beliefs.business_model: Cross-border crypto payments using XRP (On-Demand Liquidity)
beliefs.product_mix: Payments and settlement via XRP; no stablecoin, no prime brokerage, no banking
beliefs.regulatory_status: Under SEC securities litigation; MSB registrations; not a chartered bank
beliefs.ubo: Co-founders Chris Larsen and Brad Garlinghouse; venture investors; privately held
beliefs.digital_asset_policy: Holds XRP; no fiat-pegged stablecoin issued
beliefs.pep_status: No PEP among directors or UBOs
beliefs.adverse_media_status: SEC complaint pending; no other adverse media

events.summary: Ripple launches RLUSD, a US dollar-pegged stablecoin, entering the stablecoin market
Ripple acquires prime broker Hidden Road for $1.25B, expanding into institutional prime brokerage and clearing
Ripple appoints Bank of New York (BNY) as primary custodian for the RLUSD stablecoin reserves
SEC lawsuit against Ripple ends; both sides drop appeals, Ripple pays a $125M penalty for unregistered XRP sales
Ripple acquires treasury-management firm GTreasury for ~$1B and stablecoin-payments firm Rail for $200M
Ripple receives OCC conditional approval for a national trust bank charter, allowing it to operate as a federally regulated fiduciary

companies.onboarded_as: Crypto spot exchange and custody provider, private, under SEC scrutiny

beliefs.product_mix: Crypto spot trading, staking and custody; no futures, no equities
beliefs.regulatory_status: US state money-transmitter licences; under SEC scrutiny over staking; not a CFTC-registered FCM
beliefs.ubo: Co-founder Jesse Powell; venture investors; privately held
beliefs.source_of_wealth: Exchange trading fees and venture funding
beliefs.pep_status: No PEP among directors or UBOs
beliefs.adverse_media_status: SEC staking matter pending; no other adverse media

events.summary: SEC drops its lawsuit against Kraken, ending the case over its staking and exchange operations
Kraken agrees to acquire US retail futures platform NinjaTrader for $1.5B, gaining a CFTC-registered FCM licence and entering US futures
Kraken rolls out commission-free trading of over 11,000 US-listed stocks and ETFs, moving into equities
Kraken raises $800M at a $20B valuation; 2025 revenue up 33% to more than $2.2B
Kraken confidentially files for a US IPO, aiming to list as early as Q1 2026
Kraken pauses its planned IPO due to market conditions

companies.onboarded_as: Fintech e-money / neobank app, private, UK EMI (not a full bank)

beliefs.product_mix: Cards, FX, payments and some retail crypto trading; not a full deposit-taking bank
beliefs.regulatory_status: UK Electronic Money Institution + EU bank licence via Lithuania; NOT a full UK bank
beliefs.ubo: Founder Nik Storonsky (majority); SoftBank and venture investors; privately held
beliefs.digital_asset_policy: Offers retail crypto trading to customers
beliefs.pep_status: No PEP among directors or UBOs

events.summary: Revolut pays a EUR 3.5M fine to Lithuania's central bank, the largest AML fine ever in Lithuania, for anti-money-laundering compliance failures in its European banking operations
Revolut is selected by the UK FCA for a regulatory sandbox for fiat-pegged stablecoins
Revolut's full UK banking licence is held up over compliance concerns; the PRA presses the firm to bulk up its risk and financial-crime infrastructure to match its global scale
Revolut reaches a $75B valuation, up 66% year-on-year, with a projected $3.5B profit for 2026
The PRA lifts all restrictions; Revolut Bank UK Ltd becomes a fully licensed UK bank supervised by the PRA and FCA
Revolut's crypto trading arm will operate through a separate entity not covered by the FSCS deposit-protection scheme
